sessions.intent: Design test contract for purging System.IO from PLang — derivation verbs, .goal MIME, Goal/Path typing flip, AppGoals migration, action-handler denial paths, PLNG002 analyzer, and the .Absolute discipline rule.

before.assumptions: Architect plan v(final) approved by Ingi; 13 design decisions settled. Stage 1 is greenfield (no derivation verbs yet). Existing FileSystem/Surface/Permission tests give the canned-channel + root-setup pattern I'll reuse.
before.risk: Tests must define behaviour for code that doesn't yet compile (handlers still hold string Path). Signatures only — Assert.Fail bodies until coder implements.

actions.context: C# TUnit test signatures (Assert.Fail bodies) covering 11 batches mapped 1:1 onto the architect's stages 1–6. Reuses CannedChannel/StatelessChannel patterns from FileSystemPermissionFlowTests for actor + permission-prompt setup.
PLang .test.goal scaffolds (throw 'not implemented') for ring-2 handler denial paths. Follows the repo convention <Scenario>/Start.test.goal used by existing Tests/Permission/Authorize/ tests.
Session output.

One deliberate divergence from architect: pulled D13 (.Absolute discipline) out of Stage 7 (docs) and gave it its own test batch with a mutation-test placeholder. The rule is only real if a test catches a missing Authorize.

after.notes: ~80 C# test signatures + 8 PLang .test.goal scaffolds written across 11 batches. All bodies are Assert.Fail/throw — coder bot picks up next, stage order 1→7 from architect's plan.

sessions.intent: Red/blue audit of the System.IO purge: verify AuthGate is the only filesystem gate, look for new bypasses introduced by derivation verbs, the path JsonConverter, Execute verb, and Path-typed Goal serialization.

before.assumptions: Architect's hardening is structural — the only way it goes wrong is (a) a new verb that skips AuthGate, (b) a wire path that smuggles a typed path bypassing scheme/Resolve, (c) an Execute-gated load site that still reaches under the gate, or (d) the implicit string→path operator firing in an attack-reachable place.
before.risk: False sense of security — PLNG002 says 'no System.IO' at the syntactic layer, but only AuthGate enforces it semantically. A new verb that builds a FilePath from a string and calls private internal IO is the highest-leverage bypass.

actions.context: Blue-team audit of the gate surface and new verbs. Verified Assembly.LoadFrom is single-sited and Execute-gated; PathJsonConverter Context-bound routes through Resolve.

Mutation test created, run, and deleted. Verified F1 reproduces in ~5s: relative path with .. + LoadedFromPrPath inside root → IsInRoot auto-grant → read of file outside root with no prompt. Production source unchanged; no commit of the mutation.
Session output.

after.notes: F1 HIGH path-traversal in AuthGate's IsInRoot auto-grant. Bypass pre-existed the branch but is now the single chokepoint hole — branch's whole premise depends on it. Fix is structural (canonicalize _absolutePath at FilePath construction or in file.Resolve). All other surface clean: Execute verb, PathJsonConverter, derivation verbs, PLNG002 enforcement all check out.

sessions.intent: Re-review after coder's response to v1: verify F1 (HIGH), F2 (LOW latent-Medium), F3 (LOW) are genuinely closed and no new attack surface introduced by the PathHelper bridge and PLNG002 narrowing.

before.assumptions: Coder shipped 064724fda (F1 + F3) and 987a5148e (F2). F1 should be closed by canonicalizing at the FilePath ctor — preferred fix shape from v1. PathHelper is the new System.IO.Path bridge.
before.risk: Canonicalization at the ctor only matters if every construction site routes through it; need to verify Resolve, JsonConverter, derivation verbs, implicit operator, direct ctor. PathHelper introduces a new attack surface if its body accidentally adds IO.

actions.context: End-to-end trace of F1/F2/F3 fixes. Verified Canonicalize covers every FilePath entry point; the three deliberate skips (empty, non-rooted, //-prefix) reasoned safe. MarkdownTeaching lifted to path verbs and ResolveMarkdownTeachingRoot routes overrides through Resolve. PLNG002 narrowed to two carve-outs (PathHelper.cs for Path.*, app/types/path/** for File/Directory) with no whole-file exemptions.

v1 mutation reproducer ported to a permanent regression suite. Mutated Canonicalize to return absolutePath, rebuilt, ran the suite — all 3 F1 tests failed (Ctor canonicalize, Resolve+dotdot leaves root, ReadText denial). Reverted clean.
v2 session output. security-report.json updated with status:fixed + fix_commit per finding.

after.notes: All v1 findings closed. F1 mutation-verified. No new findings. Next bot: auditor.

sessions.intent: Second-opinion auditor pass: verify cross-file contracts, the green-bar tester claimed, and that PLNG002 has no suppression smuggled outside the analyzer.

before.assumptions: Three reviewers (codeanalyzer/tester/security) all said PASS at v2. Their slices were good — the gap is the seams between them.
before.risk: Stale-binary trap on a branch this size; tester reported full green-bar from a build the runner inherited, not a clean rebuild.

actions.context: Read prior bot reports to scope what I do NOT need to re-check.

Confirmed codeanalyzer N1/N4/N5 closed; PLNG002 carve-outs tight (IsPathHelperFile + IsPathTypeSurface only, no whole-file exemption); F1 canonicalization centralized in file.@this ctor; F3 OsAbsolutePath routes through PathHelper without pragma.

Clean rebuild + full-suite plang --test = 204/206. Bisect to commit 064724fda (F1 canonicalization). Runtime2 baseline = 206/206. Pre-F1 commit on branch = 206/206. Tester v2 round-2 report claims 206/206 — does not reproduce.
Auditor session output: plan, result, verdict (FAIL), structured finding, summary.

Per Ingi explicit ask: PLNG002 has exactly 2 file-scope exemptions, both inside Plng002.cs (IsPathHelperFile + IsPathTypeSurface). Zero pragma/SuppressMessage/NoWarn/editorconfig suppressions elsewhere in the tree.

after.notes: F1 closes a real HIGH-severity vuln correctly; 2 PLang tests regress because they were riding the un-canonicalized prefix-match. Fix is test-side (path or runtime-dir anchor), not a F1 rollback. PLNG002 enforcement is airtight — no suppression anywhere outside the analyzer itself.

sessions.intent: Review coder's delta since codeanalyzer v1 (security F1/F2 fixes, auditor F1 follow-up, PathHelper, MarkdownTeaching path-verb lift, App.Parent + parent-chain IsInRoot).

before.assumptions: v1 was PASS. Auditor flagged a 206→204 PLang test regression from F1; coder's last commit (bfb34bca4) adds App.Parent + parent-chain walk to fix it. Expectation: structural shape is right, edge-case knobs may be loose.
before.risk: PathHelper carve-out logic in PLNG002 must remain the only exemption; any whole-file pragma or NoWarn regresses the v1 PLNG002-airtight claim.

actions.context: Read prior bot reports to scope what I do NOT need to re-check; v1 cleanups (N1/N4/N5) already auditor-confirmed closed.

5-pass review on the v1→HEAD delta (~470/-300 LOC under PLang/ + PLang.Generators/). Verified v1 N1 closed (Json.cs alloc inside ??); PathHelper is a pure forwarder; PLNG002 carve-outs are exact-match per-finding; Canonicalize correctly skips relative and // prefixes; IsInRoot parent-chain walk earns its place (deletion-test would re-break the auditor's 2 tests); MarkdownTeaching path-verb lift is clean.

Plan + report + verdict for v2. 5 LOW findings: N1 App.Parent settable cycle guard, N2 Canonicalize bare catch, N3 IsUnder per-call string concat, N4 ValidatePath ungated File.Exists probes, N5 ScanOrphans inverted list-then-filter.

after.notes: PASS (NEEDS WORK — low only). All v1 findings closed; F1/F2/F3 structurally clean; PLNG002 carve-outs remain auditor-airtight. 5 LOW follow-ups are tighten-the-screw, not loose-the-screw.

sessions.intent: v2 review: verify v1 F1 regression closed by coder bfb34bca4 (App.Parent chain in IsInRoot); audit the new attack surface; confirm PLNG002 carve-out count unchanged.

before.assumptions: Coder claims F1 regression is closed via App.Parent. Codeanalyzer v2 passed with 5 LOW findings; N1+N2 already addressed. The Parent walk widens IsInRoot — needs scrutiny for whether it reopens security F1.
before.risk: A public-settable Parent could be a back-door for scope elevation if action handlers can reach sibling app refs. Cycle DoS already capped.

actions.context: Codeanalyzer v2 found N1 (Parent cycle) and N2 (bare catch); both addressed by coder in ecdd0de4f. Coder went stronger on N2 (removed try/catch entirely instead of filtering).

App.Parent chain walks canonicalized Absolute — F1 protection intact. Cycle cap 16 honest. No action-handler reach to attacker-controlled app refs in-process. The N2 follow-up removed the try/catch entirely.
PLNG002 still has exactly 2 carve-outs (IsPathHelperFile, IsPathTypeSurface); zero suppressions anywhere else in the tree. Re-confirmed per Ingi explicit ask.
Clean rebuild + full-suite plang --test = 206/206 (matches runtime2 baseline). v1 F1 regression closed. C# 3031/3031.
v2 auditor output: PASS verdict.

after.notes: Branch ready for docs. v1 F1 closed; no new findings. PLNG002 carve-outs unchanged (exactly 2, both inside Plng002.cs). C# 3031/3031, PLang 206/206 on clean rebuild.

sessions.intent: Final docs gate for purge-systemio-from-actions: fill XML/architecture/CHANGELOG gaps, triage claude-md + character proposals, run app-tree drift check, pass verdict if clean.

before.assumptions: Auditor v2 PASS with no findings; suite green (C# 3031/3031, PLang 206/206); branch ready for docs gate. Coder stage 7 already wrote some user-facing docs (System.IO ban + migration status) — my pass verifies completeness and fills the rest.
before.risk: Largest risk: docs miss a public surface added during the System.IO purge (new PathHelper / path verbs / Plng002 analyzer) and the merge ships an undocumented public API. Secondary risk: a CLAUDE.md proposal that doesn't generalize gets applied anyway.

actions.context: Coder stage 7 wrote the PLNG002 one-liner before stage 6 flipped severity to Error. Fixed the stale parenthetical to match landed state.
Added one-line XML doc to the two public ctors distinguishing stub (no Context) vs Context-wired forms. Class summary had the full story but call sites were bare.

Added App.Parent (new this branch), added Snapshot data partial (pre-existing omission), added paragraph explaining app.Modules.Schema is the LLM catalog object, not a registered action module. Maintenance note pointing at the new checker was carried from runtime2.

Carried from runtime2 (where it was built one chat earlier). Added Schema to the module-folder skip list. Reports drift between PLang/app/ and Documentation/v0.2/app-tree.md (modules, app.@this props, actor.@this props, Data partials). Exit 1 on drift.
Standard docs-bot output for v1. verdict.json: PASS.

coder/v1 proposal to add 'Default impl:' lines under abstract-type entries in app-tree.md → DEFERRED. The target file's shape (one-screen summary with fenced trees, no per-type sections) doesn't fit the proposed change. Right home is /shared/app-tree/ deep tree. Recorded in docs-report.json.

Comment-rot sweep across production C# (per Ingi mid-session). Stripped review-iteration / D-number / Q-number / architect-plan markers from comments. Substance preserved; provenance dropped. Builds clean (0 errors).

Same sweep across PLang.Tests/. ~50 test files updated, mostly stripping 'Stage N — Batch M.' summary prefixes. Some orphan strips required manual cleanup (broken sentences caught by re-grep + spot fix).

Removed four /shared/app-tree/ references from app-tree.md (Ingi confirmed mid-session that the shared tree is no longer maintained — in-repo app-tree.md is now canonical). Updated coder/v1 CLAUDE.md proposal decision from 'deferred' to 'rejected' since the proposal's stated right home (/shared/app-tree/) no longer exists as a maintained doc.

after.health: Branch is doc-complete. Drift checker reports clean (31 modules, 37 app props, 7 actor props, 5 data partials).
after.notes: Ready to merge into runtime2. One claude-md proposal deferred (form mismatch with target file); no character proposals on this branch. Mid-session add-ons: comment-rot sweep across production + tests (substance preserved, provenance dropped); /shared/app-tree/ references removed (no longer maintained — Ingi confirmation).
